articles.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
articles.summary: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.

articles.title: Restrictions on obesity drug coverage force patients to pivot
articles.summary: Twelve million people lost coverage for Zepbound over the last year. The same number of people lost coverage for Wegovy, according to an analysis by GoodRx, a drug discount website.

articles.title: Hoarding Is Driving Energy Prices Higher Everywhere
articles.summary: As wealthy nations scramble to secure stocks of oil, the result is higher prices for all and shortages in vulnerable countries.

articles.title: In Virginia Redistricting Win, Democrats Play Hardball to Thwart Trump
articles.summary: “We cannot bring a stick to a knife fight”: Democrats are increasingly open to extreme measures, including gerrymandering, when the stakes are high.

articles.title: To Iran, Trump Blinked First by Extending the Cease-Fire
articles.summary: Iran’s leaders believe that they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump. The strategy could be economically devastating for average Iranians.

articles.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz. And, VA voters approve redistricting effort
articles.summary: Two ships came under attack in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after Trump extended the ceasefire with Iran. And, Virginia voters approved a measure allowing Democrats to redraw the congressional map.

articles.title: Iran attacks ships in Hormuz Strait as the U.S. continues its blockade amid ceasefire
articles.summary: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump extended the ceasefire but kept the blockade.

articles.title: Democrats Win Big in Election Map Fight, and Influencers Push Nicotine as a Health Hack
articles.summary: Plus, how rainforests can bounce back.

articles.title: Abbott Uses Trump-Style Tactics, Threatens Houston, Dallas and Austin Over ICE Policy
articles.summary: Gov. Greg Abbott gave the leaders of Houston, Dallas and Austin until Wednesday to amend their policies to his liking, or return over $150 million in police funding.

articles.title: RFK Jr. is on a congressional hearing blitz, after a long absence from Capitol Hill
articles.summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. caps off seven budget hearings in as many days, the first time he has testified before Congress since September.

articles.title: Trump administration delays rule aimed at improving disability access in schools
articles.summary: Schools, colleges and other public institutions originally had until this week to make online content accessible to people with disabilities. Now, the Justice Department has delayed that deadline.

articles.title: How the airline industry could be impacted if Spirit Airlines is liquidated
articles.summary: Spirit has not confirmed a liquidation, though it has filed for bankruptcy protection twice. Experts predict rising fuel costs could push the company to close its doors for good.

articles.title: From the Himalayas to Newt Gingrich, the 'tree-huggers' prevail
articles.summary: On Earth Day, we take a look back at the rocky history of "tree-huggers." The term originated in the 1970s in the Himalayas and was later co-opted by American politics in the 1990s. Now, environmental

articles.title: Virginia Passes Gerrymandered Map to Help Democrats in Midterms: 4 Takeaways
articles.summary: Virginia’s approval of an aggressive gerrymander could give Democrats up to four additional House seats as they seek to win back Congress.

articles.title: Priceless 2,500-year-old golden helmet returned to Romania after Dutch museum raid
articles.summary: The ornate Cotofenesti helmet, which was stolen in January 2025 while on loan to a Dutch museum, was recovered last month.

articles.title: Mexico to beef up security at tourist sites after shooting at pyramids
articles.summary: Mexico's government is boosting security at tourist sites in preparation for the World Cup after a man opened fire at the Teotihuacan pyramids.

articles.title: What Was the 2015 Iran Nuclear Deal?
articles.summary: President Trump has ridiculed President Barack Obama and the agreement, which he withdrew from during his first term. His critics say he could have avoided a war had he left it in place.

articles.title: Federal Appeals Court Upholds Texas Ten Commandments Law
articles.summary: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit said the law does not violate the Constitution. The plaintiffs said they planned to ask the Supreme Court to reverse the decision.

articles.title: As Pakistan seeks to broker U.S.–Iran peace, citizens watch for gains at home
articles.summary: As Pakistan works to broker peace between the U.S. and Iran, many Pakistanis are watching closely to see whether the diplomatic boost will translate into improvements at home.

articles.title: What would happen next if Iran agreed to surrender its highly enriched uranium
articles.summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Scott Roecker of the Nuclear Threat Initiative about how the U.S. might go about moving highly enriched uranium out of Iran.

articles.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
articles.summary: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
